root_cause: Hottest-spot winding temperature has reached the IEEE C57.91 alarm threshold (130 °C) while top-oil temperature remains within nominal range. This thermal divergence is characteristic of a localized winding hot spot — most often caused by a turn-to-turn insulation defect, a loose connection, or partial blockage of a single oil duct.

recommended_actions.action: Reduce load to ≤ 50% of rated kVA immediately and notify the duty operator.
recommended_actions.rationale: A localized winding hot spot can transition to a flashover with minutes of warning. Load reduction lowers I²R heat generation in the affected winding.

recommended_actions.action: Pull a DGA sample within the next 24 hours and request fast-turn-around laboratory analysis.
recommended_actions.rationale: DGA gas ratios (Duval triangle) discriminate between thermal faults < 300 °C, > 700 °C, and electrical arcing — required to plan the correct intervention.

recommended_actions.action: Plan a controlled outage for thermographic and electrical-test inspection within 14 days.
recommended_actions.rationale: If DGA confirms thermal fault, the asset can remain de-rated until inspection. If DGA shows arcing, immediate removal from service is required.
